title: Entity Pattern -- NXGN Actions
purpose: **AI Summary**: Domain model base classes, interfaces, and entity conventions for the Actions platform. Covers `Entity`, `TrackedEntity`, `ITenantEntity`, `ITranslatedEntity<T>`, `ITranslation`, `EntityTranslation`, `ISoftDeletable`, `IVersioned`, primary keys, collections, foreign keys, enum properties, and constructors. For EF Core configuration (IEntityTypeConfiguration, indexes, column types, delete behavior, RLS), see [EF Configuration](ef-configuration.md).

examples.name: Base Classes and Interfaces Example 1
examples.code:
IEntity
  └── Entity               (Guid Id)
        └── TrackedEntity   (+ CreatedDate, CreatedById, UpdatedDate, UpdatedById)
              └── EntityTranslation  (abstract, + ITenantEntity, ITranslation)

ITenantEntity              (TenantId: Guid)
ITranslatedEntity<T>       (Guid Id + IList<T> Translations)
ITranslation               (string Name, string? Description, Guid LanguageId)
ISoftDeletable             (bool IsDeleted, DateTime? DeletedDate, Guid? DeletedById)
IVersioned                 (int Version)

sections.title: Base Classes and Interfaces
content: ITranslation               (string Name, string? Description, Guid LanguageId)
ISoftDeletable             (bool IsDeleted, DateTime? DeletedDate, Guid? DeletedById)
| `Entity` | Lightweight join/value entities with no audit trail | `WorkflowStateTransition`, `ActionTag` |
| `TrackedEntity` | Aggregate roots and entities needing audit fields | `ActionEntity`, `WorkflowDefinition`, `ComplianceProfile`, `EscalationRule` |
Entities scoped to a single tenant implement `ITenantEntity`. RLS policies filter by `TenantId` at the database level -- application code does not filter by tenant.

Entities that should never be physically deleted implement `ISoftDeletable`. Delete commands set these fields instead of removing rows. Global query filters in EF Core exclude soft-deleted records by default.

Entities that need optimistic concurrency control implement `IVersioned`. The `Version` field is configured as a concurrency token in EF Core. Save commands check the version to prevent conflicting updates.
public interface ITranslatedEntity<T> where T : ITranslation
public abstract class EntityTranslation : TrackedEntity, ITenantEntity, ITranslation
public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public Language Language { get; set; } = null!;

Entities that need translation implement `ITranslatedEntity<T>` where `T` extends `EntityTranslation`. Each translation record carries a `LanguageId` FK and tenant scope. The `TranslationService` handles fallback resolution (request language -> default -> first available). Actions supports translations from day one.

sections.title: Entity Conventions
content: Required FK -- both ID and navigation with `= null!`:
public WorkflowDefinition WorkflowDefinition { get; set; } = null!;
Optional FK -- nullable ID and nullable navigation:

All status, kind, and mode fields use C# enums -- never `static class` with `const string`. Enums provide compile-time safety, exhaustive `switch` checking, and `HasConversion<string>()` in EF Core for readable database values.
public ActionStatus Status { get; set; } = ActionStatus.Open;
public EscalationSeverity Severity { get; set; } = EscalationSeverity.Medium;
public WorkflowTriggerType TriggerType { get; set; } = WorkflowTriggerType.Manual;
public string Status { get; set; } = ActionStatuses.Open;  // violates ADR-0004
Define enums in the Contracts project so DTOs can reference them for `EnumDTO` mapping:
Entities use the default parameterless constructor (class-level properties with initializers). Primary constructors are for services, commands, queries, and mappers -- not entities.
Use `JsonDocument` for semi-structured fields that vary per tenant or don't warrant dedicated columns:
public JsonDocument Configuration { get; set; } = JsonDocument.Parse("{}");
public JsonDocument Metadata { get; set; } = JsonDocument.Parse("{}");
These require custom value converters in EF configuration (see [EF Configuration](ef-configuration.md)).

sections.title: Entity Examples
content: ### Aggregate Root with Translations and Soft Delete
public class ActionEntity : TrackedEntity, ITenantEntity, ISoftDeletable, IVersioned
public string ReferenceNumber { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public ActionStatus Status { get; set; } = ActionStatus.Open;
public ActionPriority Priority { get; set; } = ActionPriority.Medium;
public JsonDocument Metadata { get; set; } = JsonDocument.Parse("{}");
public WorkflowDefinition WorkflowDefinition { get; set; } = null!;
public class WorkflowDefinition : TrackedEntity, ITenantEntity, ITranslatedEntity<WorkflowDefinitionTranslation>, IVersioned
public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public bool IsActive { get; set; } = true;
public JsonDocument Configuration { get; set; } = JsonDocument.Parse("{}");
public WorkflowDefinition WorkflowDefinition { get; set; } = null!;
public ActionEntity Action { get; set; } = null!;
public Tag Tag { get; set; } = null!;
public string Code { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public string Name { get; set; } = string.Empty;
public bool IsActive { get; set; } = true;
